NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN,SNAPPED,OR UPROOTED ON LAKE VERNON ROAD, WEST HAWTHORNE ROAD, US HWY 171, LA HWY 117, LA HWY 28, AND BELVIEW ROAD. SOME OF THESE TREES LANDED ON HOMES AND VEHICLES. NO MAJOR INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

MULTIPLE REPORTS OF TREES DOWN AND DAMAGE IN THE BEECH GROVE AREA NORTH OF HWY190.

THE TORNADO DEVELOPED ALONG COUNTY ROAD 278 EAST OF JASPER, TEXAS. THE STORM PRODUCED DAMAGE TO ROOFSAND HOMES, AND SNAPPED TREES ALONG ITS PATH. THE TORNADO DEBARKED TREES AT TIMES, ALONG ITS PATH. IT DISSIPATED NEARHWY 171 NORTH OF THE COMMUNITY OF ROSEPINE, LOUISIANA. NO ONE WAS SERIOUSLY INJURED.
TREES DOWN, SOME ON POWERLINES IN MULTIPLE LOCATIONS ACROSS CENTRAL JASPER.
ROOF BLOWN OFF MOBILE HOME AND TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ON ROADWAYS.
LARGE HAIL NEAR FARRSVILLE ESTIMATED 2.5 IN
TREES DOWN ACROSS SECTIONS OF HWY 87.

THIS TORNADO STARTED IN THE COMMUNITY OF ELMER, WEST OF ELMER ROAD WHERE IT PRODUCED DAMAGE TOTREES AND HOMES. THE TORNADO CROSSED HWY 112 SE OF ELMER AND CONTINUED NE THROUGH THE FOREST, DAMAGING MANY TREESAND POWER POLES ALONG ITS PATH. THE PATH WAS LOST NEAR CASTOR PLUNGE ROAD IN THE FOREST. THE TRACK MAY BE ADJUSTED PENDING ADDITIONAL DATA.
LARGE TREES UPROOTED/SNAPPED, ROOF BLOWN OFF, AND POWERLINES DOWN.

*** 1 FATAL *** A STRONG LONG TRACK SUPERCELL WITH A HISTORY OF PRODUCING TORNADOES TRACKED THROUGH CENTRAL RAPIDES PARISH LATE WEDNESDAY EVENING. THE CIRCULATION TRACKED ENE PAST WOODWORTH, RESULTING IN 1 FATALITY ALONG ROBINSON BRIDGE RD. FORTHCOMING SURVEYS WILL ASSESS THE TRACK AND STRENGTH OF THE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

*** 1 FATAL *** THIS TORNADO STARTED NEAR I-49 AND ROBINSONBRIDGE ROAD, WHERE IT DAMAGED NUMEROUS HOMES AND TREES ALONGTHE ROAD. TWO MOBILE HOMES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED, KILLINGONE INDIVIDUAL. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED ENE CROSSING HWY 470 DAMAGING ADDITIONAL HOMES AND BRINGING DOWN NUMEROUS POWERPOLES. THE TORNADO THEN JOGGED NE CROSSING HWY 71 WHERE IT PRODUCED SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE TO THE DEWITT LIVESTOCK FACILITY. THE TORNADO ALSO SUBSTANTIALLY DAMAGED FARM AND EQUIPMENT BUILDINGS AT THE LSUA DEAN LEE RESE

TORNADO STARTED EAST OF CHICKAMAW ROADIN AN OPEN FIELD THEN DAMAGED A SHED AND TOOK SHINGLESOFF A ROOF ON HWY 456 BEFORE CROSSING HWY 71. THERE IT DAMAGED ANOTHER BUILDING BEFORE CROSSING LAMOURIE ROAD.IT DAMAGED OUT BUILDINGS AND SNAPPED POWER LINES. IT DISSIPATED NORTH OF LAMOURIE ROAD IN A FIELD.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS AT LSUA.

TORNADO STARTED W OF HWY 454 AND CROSSED THEHWY MOVING ENE ALONG AND NORTH OF WISE CHAPEL ROAD. IT PRODUCED CONSIDERABLE TREE DAMAGE WHICH RESULTED IN A FEW HOMES BEING DAMAGED AS WELL. THE TORNADO TRACK WAS LOST VOOHRIES ROAD BEFORE CROSSING INTO AVOYELLES PARISH.
3-INCH TREE LIMBS BROKEN AND POWER POLES BROKEN.

IT TRAVELED ALONG THE CATAHOULA PARISH / AVOYELLES PARISH BORDER, WHERE IT DAMAGED TREES AND MOBILES ALONGLULA WILLEY ROAD. IT THEN CROSSED THE LAKE OPHELIA NATIONAL WILDLIFE REFUGE BEFORE CROSSING BACK INTO CATAHOULA PARISH.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL LASTING ABOUT 10 MINUTES
NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTS 3/4 TO 1 INCH SIZE HAIL FALLING FROM 1249 TO 1254 AM. ALSO TREE LIMB KNOCKED DOWN BY ESTIMATED 50 MPH WINDS.

THE TORNADO STARTED EAST OF HWYS 115 AND 1176 W OF BUNKIE, PRODUCING TREE DAMAGE. IT CROSSED THE BUNKIE AIRPORTWHERE IT PRODUCED CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE TO HANGARS AND AIRCRAFT. IT CONTINUED ENE AND DISSIPATED NEAR HWY 171 ON THE NORTH SIDE OFBUNKIE.
STRONG WIND DAMAGE REPORTED BY NWS EMPLOYEE DRIVING THROUGH FENTON AREA. TORNADO POSSIBLE, TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

TORNADO STARTED W OF HWY 29 AND CROSSED THE HWYWHERE IT PRODUCED DAMAGE TO HOMES AND OUT BUILDINGS. TORNADO DISSIPATED SHORTLY BEFORE REACHING CRACKVILLE ROAD.

THE TORNADO STARTED ON GRAND COULEE ROAD AS IT PRODUCED TREE AND POWER LINE DAMAGE ALONG MOST OF THE PATH. HOWEVER, MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS MINIMIZED DUE TO THE TRACK GOING THROUGH MANY OPEN FIELDS. A LARGE SHED WAS DAMAGED ALONG RUPERT LAKE ROAD.
